meta.title: Piazza del Popolo, Ascoli Piceno - Tourism Guide
meta.description: Independent tourism guide to Piazza del Popolo, the Renaissance heart of Ascoli Piceno. Discover history, architecture, reviews, and tips for your visit.

hero.title: Piazza del Popolo
hero.subtitle: The Renaissance drawing room of Ascoli Piceno

intro.title: Discover the Square
intro.description: Piazza del Popolo is the beating heart of Ascoli Piceno, one of the most elegant squares in Italy. Entirely paved in travertine, it is surrounded by Renaissance buildings with harmonious porticoes.
highlights: Entirely clad in local travertine stone
Palazzo dei Capitani del Popolo (13th century)
Historic Caffè Meletti, an Art Nouveau gem

photos.caption: Panoramic view of the square at sunset

reviews.title: Reviews from Real Visitors
reviews.declaration: Ratings and reviews sourced from Google Maps (last updated: 2026). We only display a selection of verified high-scoring reviews. For all complete and most recent reviews, click the link below.
reviews.moreLink: See all reviews on Google Maps

items.name: Marco R.
items.text: One of the most beautiful squares in Italy. The atmosphere at sunset is magical, with the illuminated porticoes and the travertine turning golden.

items.name: Giulia S.
items.text: Wonderful square! Caffè Meletti is a must-visit — try the anisetta. The Renaissance architecture is perfectly preserved.

items.name: Thomas K.
items.text: Incredible that this square is so little known outside Italy. It easily rivals the most famous piazzas.

items.name: Yuki T.
items.text: The white travertine makes everything bright and elegant. The porticoes offer shelter from both sun and rain. Perfect in every season.

items.name: Alessandro M.
items.text: Beautiful square, a gathering point for all locals. The Quintana festival in August transforms the piazza into a medieval stage.

items.name: Sophie L.
items.text: We spent hours sitting at the outdoor tables admiring the architecture. Palazzo dei Capitani is imposing, and the civic tower dominates the square.

items.name: Hans W.
items.text: This square is a masterpiece of architectural harmony. Every building blends perfectly with the others. An absolute must-visit.

items.name: Chiara B.
items.text: During the Quintana, the square comes alive with medieval colours. But even on an ordinary day, it is a place of extraordinary peace and beauty.

visitDuration.title: How long does a visit take?
visitDuration.text: A visit to the square takes about 1-2 hours. Allow extra time for a coffee at Meletti and to explore the surrounding streets of the historic centre.

worthIt.title: Is it worth visiting?
worthIt.text: Absolutely. Piazza del Popolo is considered one of the most beautiful squares in Italy, often compared to Piazza San Marco for its architectural harmony.

whatInside.title: What can you find in the square?
items: Palazzo dei Capitani del Popolo - seat of civic power since the 13th century
Church of San Francesco - Gothic, dominating the western side
Caffè Meletti - historic Art Nouveau café (since 1907)
Numerous bars and restaurants under the porticoes

tips.title: Practical Tips
items: Visit at sunset for the best light on the travertine
The Quintana festival (first Sunday of August) is spectacular
Try olive ascolane at the trattorias in the square
The historic centre is pedestrianised, easy to explore on foot
Parking is available on the outskirts of the old town

map.title: How to Get There
map.address: Piazza del Popolo, 63100 Ascoli Piceno AP, Italy
